title_en: Ash-Shahrastani: Theologian and Pioneering Scholar of Religions
title_id: Ash-Shahrastani: Teolog dan Pelopor Kajian Agama-Agama
content_en:
Abu al-Fath Muhammad ibn Abd al-Karim ash-Shahrastani (479–548 AH / 1086–1153 CE) was an Ash'ari theologian and Shafi'i jurist from Shahrastan in Khorasan (in the region of present-day Turkmenistan) who produced one of the most remarkable works in the history of Islamic intellectual scholarship: Al-Milal wan-Nihal, a systematic survey of the religious and philosophical beliefs of the world's major traditions.

Ash-Shahrastani received his education in Khorasan and later in Nishapur, then one of the great centers of Islamic learning in the eastern Islamic world. He mastered the Shafi'i legal tradition, Ash'ari theology, Islamic philosophy, and the literature of comparative religion. His teachers included scholars of the post-Ghazali generation, and his work shows the influence of al-Ghazali's philosophical sophistication.

He served in administrative and scholarly capacities in Khorasan and traveled to Baghdad, where he reportedly gave lectures at the Nizamiyyah madrasa — the prestigious institution that had hosted al-Ghazali a generation earlier. His scholarly reputation was high in his own time, and later scholars treated him as a significant authority on the history of Islamic theology and comparative religion.

Ash-Shahrastani's theological positioning has been a subject of scholarly debate. While he is conventionally classified as Ash'ari in theology and Shafi'i in law, some scholars have detected Ismaili or Batini sympathies in some of his writings, and his commentary on the Quran reflects some distinctive theological positions. These questions do not diminish his scholarly achievement in Al-Milal wan-Nihal but remind readers to approach his presentations of various schools with critical awareness.

His other significant works include Nihayat al-Aqdam fi Ilm al-Kalam, a comprehensive treatise on Islamic theology (kalam), and a commentary on certain books of Ibn Sina's philosophy. These works, alongside Al-Milal wan-Nihal, establish him as one of the most philosophically sophisticated scholars of his generation. The breadth of ash-Shahrastani's reading — spanning Islamic law, kalam theology, philosophy, and the comparative study of non-Islamic traditions — was unusual even in an era known for polymathic scholars, and it equipped him uniquely for the task of surveying the world's religious and intellectual traditions with both accuracy and analytical depth. His work remains a starting point for anyone interested in how classical Islamic scholarship approached the diversity of human religious experience.
content_id:
Abu al-Fath Muhammad ibn Abd al-Karim ash-Shahrastani (479–548 H / 1086–1153 M) adalah seorang teolog mazhab Ash'ariyyah dan ahli fikih mazhab Syafi'i yang berasal dari Shahrastan di Khorasan — wilayah yang kini masuk dalam kawasan Turkmenistan. Beliau menghasilkan salah satu karya paling luar biasa dalam sejarah intelektual Islam: Al-Milal wan-Nihal, sebuah survei sistematis mengenai kepercayaan agama dan filsafat dari berbagai tradisi besar dunia.

Ash-Shahrastani menempuh pendidikannya di Khorasan, kemudian di Nishapur — salah satu pusat keilmuan Islam terbesar di dunia Islam bagian timur pada masanya. Beliau menguasai tradisi fikih Syafi'i, akidah Ash'ariyyah, filsafat Islam, serta literatur perbandingan agama. Para gurunya termasuk ulama dari generasi setelah Al-Ghazali, dan karya-karyanya mencerminkan pengaruh kecanggihan filsafat Al-Ghazali yang mendalam.

Beliau menjalankan berbagai tugas administratif dan keilmuan di Khorasan, lalu melakukan perjalanan ke Baghdad, di mana beliau dilaporkan menyampaikan kuliah di Madrasah Nizamiyyah — lembaga bergengsi yang satu generasi sebelumnya pernah menampung Al-Ghazali. Reputasi keilmuannya sangat tinggi pada masanya, dan ulama-ulama setelahnya menganggap beliau sebagai otoritas penting dalam sejarah teologi Islam dan perbandingan agama.

Posisi teologis ash-Shahrastani telah menjadi subjek perdebatan di kalangan ulama. Meskipun beliau secara lazim digolongkan sebagai Ash'ari dalam akidah dan Syafi'i dalam fikih, sebagian ulama mendeteksi adanya kecenderungan Ismaili atau Bathiniyyah dalam beberapa tulisannya, dan tafsir Al-Qurannya mencerminkan beberapa posisi teologis yang khas. Pertanyaan-pertanyaan ini tidak mengurangi pencapaian ilmiahnya dalam Al-Milal wan-Nihal, namun mengingatkan para pembaca untuk mendekati pemaparan beliau tentang berbagai mazhab dengan kesadaran kritis yang memadai.

Karya-karyanya yang signifikan lainnya mencakup Nihayat al-Aqdam fi Ilm al-Kalam — sebuah risalah komprehensif tentang teologi Islam (ilmu kalam) — serta komentar terhadap sejumlah buku filsafat Ibn Sina. Karya-karya ini, bersama Al-Milal wan-Nihal, menjadikan beliau salah satu ulama yang paling canggih secara filosofis pada zamannya. Keluasan bacaan ash-Shahrastani — yang mencakup fikih Islam, teologi kalam, filsafat, dan kajian komparatif tradisi non-Islam — merupakan hal yang tidak biasa bahkan di era yang dikenal dengan para sarjana multidisiplin, dan hal itu membekali beliau secara unik untuk tugas mensurvei tradisi-tradisi agama dan intelektual dunia dengan akurasi dan kedalaman analitik yang tinggi. Karya beliau tetap menjadi titik awal bagi siapa pun yang tertarik pada bagaimana keilmuan Islam klasik mendekati keragaman pengalaman keagamaan manusia.
